title: मेहंदीपुर बालाजी आरती
devanagariText:
॥ श्री मेहंदीपुर बालाजी आरती ॥

आरती कीजै बालाजी की।
मेहंदीपुर के श्री हनुमत की॥

सिन्दूर लाल चोला, हाथ में गदा विशाला।
भूत प्रेत सब काँपें, देख तेरा ज्वाला।
दुष्ट दलन करनेवाले, रक्षक बलवाना।
मेहंदीपुर में विराजत, बालाजी महाना॥
आरती कीजै बालाजी की॥ १॥

प्रेतराज का भय हरो, संकटमोचन नाम।
भैरव बाबा संग में, करो भक्त का काम।
भक्त पुकारे द्वार पर, दौड़े आओ धाम।
बाधा कोटि निवारो, बालाजी गुणधाम॥
आरती कीजै बालाजी की॥ २॥

जो कोई दर पर आवे, खाली नहीं जाये।
मन में जो भी चाहे, सो फल पाये।
बालाजी की महिमा, कोई कह न पाये।
भक्तन की सब बाधा, आकर दूर कराये॥
आरती कीजै बालाजी की॥ ३॥

सन्तान सुख दीजै, रोगी करो निरोग।
दुष्ट ग्रह सब नाशो, काटो सर्व वियोग।
मेहंदीपुर वाले बालाजी, सदा सहाय होय।
जय बालाजी, जय बालाजी,
तेरा भक्त न कोई दुखी होय॥
आरती कीजै बालाजी की।
मेहंदीपुर के श्री हनुमत की॥ ४॥

॥ इति श्री मेहंदीपुर बालाजी आरती सम्पूर्ण ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Mehandipur Balaji Aarti ||

Aarti Kijai Balaji Ki.
Mehandipur Ke Shri Hanumat Ki.

Sindur Lal Chola, Hath Mein Gada Vishala.
Bhut Pret Sab Kanpen, Dekh Tera Jwala.
Dushta Dalana Karnewale, Rakshak Balvana.
Mehandipur Mein Virajat, Balaji Mahana.
Aarti Kijai Balaji Ki. ||1||

Pretaraj Ka Bhaya Haro, Sankat-Mochana Nama.
Bhairava Baba Sanga Mein, Karo Bhakta Ka Kama.
Bhakta Pukare Dvar Par, Daude Aao Dhama.
Badha Koti Nivaro, Balaji Gunadhama.
Aarti Kijai Balaji Ki. ||2||

Jo Koi Dar Par Aave, Khali Nahin Jaye.
Mana Mein Jo Bhi Chahe, So Phala Paye.
Balaji Ki Mahima, Koi Kaha Na Paye.
Bhaktana Ki Sab Badha, Akar Dur Karaye.
Aarti Kijai Balaji Ki. ||3||

Santan Sukh Dijai, Rogi Karo Niroga.
Dushta Graha Sab Nasho, Kato Sarva Viyoga.
Mehandipur Wale Balaji, Sada Sahaya Hoya.
Jaya Balaji, Jaya Balaji,
Tera Bhakta Na Koi Dukhi Hoya.
Aarti Kijai Balaji Ki.
Mehandipur Ke Shri Hanumat Ki. ||4||

|| Iti Shri Mehandipur Balaji Aarti Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning: मेहंदीपुर के श्री हनुमान बालाजी की आरती। सिन्दूर लाल चोला पहने, हाथ में विशाल गदा लिये — भूत-प्रेत सब काँपते हैं उनकी ज्वाला देखकर। दुष्ट दलन करने वाले बलवान रक्षक मेहंदीपुर में विराजमान हैं। प्रेतराज का भय हरने वाले संकटमोचन, भैरव बाबा के संग भक्तों का कार्य सिद्ध करते हैं। जो दरबार में आता है खाली नहीं जाता, मनोकामना पूर्ण होती है। सन्तान सुख, रोग निवारण, दुष्ट ग्रहों का नाश — बालाजी सदा सहायक हैं, उनका भक्त कभी दुखी नहीं होता।
benefits: Principal aarti of Mehandipur Balaji — the most famous shrine for removal of evil afflictions
Powerful protection against negative energies, evil eye, and malefic planetary effects
Invokes Hanuman as Balaji in His fierce protective form alongside Bhairav Baba
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of types of badha (spiritual disturbances) and grants fearlessness
Traditionally associated with relief from unexplained ailments and chronic distress
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of heartfelt wishes of devotees who visit or recite with faith
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Hanuman, Mehandipur Balaji Aarti (मेहंदीपुर बालाजी आरती) stands as a traditional worship song (aarti). Attributed to Mehandipur Balaji Temple Tradition, Rajasthan, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to fulfill heartfelt wishes of devotees who visit or recite with faith and bring relief from unexplained ailments and chronic distress. It is also said to invoke Hanuman as Balaji in His fierce protective form alongside Bhairav Baba. This sacred text is ideally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Hanuman Jayanti for enhanced blessings. The text contains 4 verses and takes around 4 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Mehandipur Balaji Aarti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Mehandipur Balaji Aarti with Hindi meaning. Powerful Hanuman aarti for protection from evil spirits and negative energies.
relatedSearches: mehandipur balaji ki aarti in hindi

seoFaqs.question: Where is the Mehandipur Balaji temple and which deities are worshipped there?
seoFaqs.answer: Shri Mehandipur Balaji Mandir is in Mehandipur village, Dausa district, Rajasthan, on the Jaipur-Agra highway. Although the presiding deity is Lord Hanuman (locally addressed as 'Balaji'), the temple is actually a triad: Balaji (Hanuman) sits in the main sanctum, Pretraj Sarkar — the 'king of ghosts' — receives offerings in a separate shrine, and Bhairav Baba (Kotwal) acts as the temple's protective kotwal. The aarti 'Om Jai Hanumat Veera' is sung primarily for Balaji, but the daily worship cycle includes obeisance to all three. The combination of these three deities is believed to give the temple its unique reputation for spiritual healing and the removal of negative influences. (Source: Lord Hanuman; Holidify)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Mehandipur Balaji known as a temple of exorcism?
seoFaqs.answer: For centuries, Mehandipur Balaji has been associated with the relief of those believed to be afflicted by 'sankat' — interpreted in popular tradition as possession by spirits, the effects of black magic, or chronic mental and physical distress. Ritual relief is said to be granted through Pretraj Sarkar, who as 'king of ghosts' commands lower spirits to depart. Devotees offer arzi (a written or symbolic petition) of urad dal and laddoos, and the priests perform havans and arti. The temple does not encourage modern medical replacement; many families combine medical treatment with these rituals. The atmosphere during peak hours can be intense, and the temple administration has published strict rules about not bringing prashad home. (Source: Holidify; Hinduism Facts)

seoFaqs.question: What are the strict rules devotees must follow at Mehandipur Balaji?
seoFaqs.answer: The temple authorities and longstanding tradition prescribe several rules to maintain the sanctity of the rituals. Devotees should not eat any non-vegetarian food, garlic, or onion before darshan; alcohol is strictly prohibited. The 'darkhast' and 'arzi' prashad must be left at the temple — it should never be carried home or eaten outside. After darshan, devotees are advised not to look back as they leave the temple complex. Photographing the inner sanctum is forbidden. Many devotees also observe a fast on the day of the visit. These rules emerge from local tradition built up over generations and are intended to keep the spiritual transactions of the temple contained within its precincts. (Source: Hanuman Chalisa Lyric Mehandipur Rules)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the opening line 'Om Jai Hanumat Veera'?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening invocation — 'Om Jai Hanumat Veera, Swami Jai Hanumat Veera / Sankat mochan swami, tum ho randhira' — translates as 'Victory to the brave Hanuman, Lord of the courageous; remover of all troubles, you are the warrior-hero.' The aarti continues by addressing Balaji as the protector who shields devotees from fear, ghosts, and afflictions, and as the swift servant of Rama who carries every petition to the divine throne. The melody is set to the same dhun as the universal Vishnu aarti 'Om Jai Jagdish Hare,' which makes it instantly singable for first-time pilgrims. The aarti is performed multiple times daily during scheduled darshan hours. (Source: Hindu Mirror; ServDharm)
